name: Antigua Mina de Llumeres, Cabo Peñas, Avilés, Tresona, Valle del Río Pervera, Luanco – circular por la costa
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Gozón, located in Asturias, Spain, offer diverse terrain along its dramatic coastline. The region features prominent cliffs, wild beaches, and green mountains meeting the Cantabrian Sea. Riders can expect varied landscapes, from coastal paths with sea views to inland sections with natural features. The area provides a mix of unpaved and challenging segments suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Gozón The most popular mountain bike trail is Luanco, Playa de Xagó, Cabo Peñas – circular por la costa, a 26.6 miles (42.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 32 minutes to complete. This route offers continuous coastal views, including the iconic Cabo Peñas. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Antigua Mina de Llumeres, Cabo Peñas, Avilés, Tresona, Valle del Río Pervera, Luanco – circular por la costa, a moderate 39.5 miles (63.5 km) path. This route explores varied coastal terrain and passes by the Cabo Peñas lighthouse. Local mountain bikers also love the Playa de Moniello loop from Ayuntamiento de Gozón, a 21.5 miles (34.6 km) trail leading through coastal areas and green spaces, often completed in about 2 hours 43 minutes. Mountain biking around Gozón is defined by spectacular cliffs, coastal routes, and green mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to those requiring advanced skills. The routes in Gozón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 1,000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Gozón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Gozón?
A: Gozón offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 110 routes available for exploration. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging adventures.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Gozón?
A: Mountain biking in Gozón features diverse terrain, characterized by dramatic coastal landscapes, prominent cliffs, and green mountains meeting the Cantabrian Sea. You'll find a mix of unpaved and challenging segments, with routes offering continuous coastal views and inland sections through natural areas. Some parts may require advanced riding skills or even pushing your bike.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails for all skill levels in Gozón?
A: Yes, Gozón provides mountain bike trails suitable for various skill levels. There are 12 easy routes for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride, 71 moderate trails for intermediate riders, and 29 difficult routes for experienced mountain bikers looking for a challenge.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Gozón?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the Cantabrian Sea and pass by significant landmarks. You can ride past the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4016988" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo Peñas Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, the northernmost point of Asturias, known for its dramatic cliffs. Other routes might take you near wild beaches like Verdicio, Xagó, Viodo, and Llumeres, or through green spaces like the Moniello Recreation Area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/391360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campa Torres Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is another notable spot offering panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in Gozón?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Gozón are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027484315/luanco-playa-de-xago-cabo-penas-circular-por-la-costa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luanco, Playa de Xagó, Cabo Peñas – circular por la costa&lt;/a&gt; route offers continuous coastal views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027486763/antigua-mina-de-llumeres-cabo-penas-aviles-tresona-valle-del-rio-pervera-luanco-circular-por-la-costa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Antigua Mina de Llumeres, Cabo Peñas, Avilés, Tresona, Valle del Río Pervera, Luanco – circular por la costa&lt;/a&gt; is another popular circular option exploring varied coastal terrain.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Gozón?
A: Gozón, being in Asturias, generally experiences a mild climate. Spring and autumn are often ideal for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Summer can also be great, especially for coastal rides, but be mindful of potential heat and crowds. Winter rides are possible, but some trails might be wet or muddy.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Gozón?
A: While many trails in Gozón can be challenging, there are 12 easy routes that could be suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. These routes typically feature gentler gradients and less technical terrain, allowing for a more relaxed experience. Always check the route details for specific difficulty and elevation before heading out with family.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Gozón?
A: The mountain bike trails in Gozón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders frequently praise the spectacular coastal views, the diverse terrain that keeps rides interesting, and the opportunity to explore the wild, untamed beauty where green mountains meet the sea.

Q: Is parking available near the mountain bike trails in Gozón?
A: While specific parking information varies by trail, many popular starting points for routes in Gozón, such as those near Luanco or the Ayuntamiento de Gozón, typically have public parking facilities available. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for the most convenient parking options.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Gozón?
A: While many outdoor areas in Asturias are dog-friendly, it's important to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas. Generally, if dogs are permitted, they should be kept under control, often on a leash, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice responsible pet ownership and clean up after your dog.

Q: Are there any routes that specifically explore the Cabo Peñas area?
A: Yes, several routes focus on the stunning Cabo Peñas area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40314687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo Peñas 🌊 – Cabo Peñas Lighthouse loop from Candás Apeaderu&lt;/a&gt; is a great option that takes you directly to the lighthouse and offers incredible coastal vistas. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027484315/luanco-playa-de-xago-cabo-penas-circular-por-la-costa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luanco, Playa de Xagó, Cabo Peñas – circular por la costa&lt;/a&gt;, also prominently features Cabo Peñas.

Q: What is a good moderate mountain bike route to experience the coast and green spaces?
A: For a moderate ride that combines coastal areas with green spaces, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15101154" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Moniello loop from Ayuntamiento de Gozón&lt;/a&gt;. This 34.6 km (21.5 miles) trail offers a balanced experience of Gozón's diverse landscapes and typically takes around 2 hours 43 minutes to complete.
tips: An iron mine that already worked in Roman times and until 1967. Now practically only ruins remain. A small recreational area and a viewpoint have been built at the entrance to the descent to the beach.
